title: AI jobs spread well beyond Silicon Valley
excerpt: AI is spreading beyond tech as employers add AI skills to jobs in healthcare, HR, sales, education, trucking, and more across the US and Europe.
content_html:
AI is everyone's job now

By Alistair Barr

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

Author of the Tech Memo newsletter

AI is escaping the tech industry. The latest evidence suggests the technology is no longer confined to software engineers and data scientists; it's spreading into the broader economy — even in Europe.

New data from Indeed shows employers are increasingly adding AI directly into job titles for non-tech roles in sales, HR, legal, customer support, education, healthcare, and even trucking.

In the US, the number of "AI-touched" job titles has more than tripled since 2022, while in five of six major markets, most AI-labeled jobs are now outside the tech sector.

One reason this matters is that more employers are making AI a core part of the job itself. Indeed found that in five of the six markets it studied, most AI-labeled roles are now outside the technology sector, with Germany, France, the UK, and the Netherlands all joining the US in seeing AI spread across white-collar professions. Spain was the only exception, where AI hiring remains concentrated in traditional tech jobs.

The shift is showing up in surprisingly familiar occupations. Employers are advertising for AI-enabled physical therapists, truck drivers, HR managers, marketers, salespeople, and teachers, reflecting demand for workers who can use AI tools rather than build them.

That's an important distinction: the AI economy is increasingly about augmenting existing jobs, not just creating new technical ones — a sign that AI adoption is becoming a broad workplace phenomenon rather than a Silicon Valley story.

Sign up for BI's Tech Memo newsletter here. Reach out to me via email at abarr@businessinsider.com.

sentences.en: Careers
sentences.zh: 职业机会

content_html_zh:
人工智能现在是每个人的工作

作者：Alistair Barr 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。 《技术备忘录》时事通讯的作者

人工智能正在逃离科技行业。 最新证据表明，这项技术不再局限于软件工程师和数据科学家；它正在蔓延到更广泛的经济领域——甚至在欧洲。

Indeed 的新数据显示，雇主越来越多地将人工智能直接添加到销售、人力资源、法律、客户支持、教育、医疗保健甚至货运等非技术职位的职位中。

在美国，自 2022 年以来，“与人工智能相关”的职位数量增加了两倍多，而在六个主要市场中的五个中，大多数带有人工智能标签的职位现在都在科技行业之外。

这很重要的原因之一是越来越多的雇主将人工智能作为工作本身的核心部分。 Indeed 发现，在其研究的六个市场中的五个市场中，大多数带有人工智能标签的角色现在都在技术领域之外，德国、法国、英国和荷兰都与美国一样，看到人工智能在白领职业中蔓延。 西班牙是唯一的例外，那里的人工智能招聘仍然集中在传统科技工作岗位。

这种转变出现在令人惊讶的熟悉职业中。 雇主们正在为支持人工智能的物理治疗师、卡车司机、人力资源经理、营销人员、销售人员和教师做广告，反映出对能够使用人工智能工具而不是构建人工智能工具的工人的需求。

这是一个重要的区别：人工智能经济越来越注重增加现有就业岗位，而不仅仅是创造新技术——这表明人工智能的采用正在成为一种广泛的工作场所现象，而不是硅谷的故事。
